R0 → R5 · evidence-state coordination

Route the institution, not the swarm.

Large agent systems become useful when GoalOS selects the smallest sufficient constellation for a mission, binds every role to tool scope, assigns validators, defines stopping rules, and exports a replayable RoutingReceipt.

Browser-local demonstration only. Do not enter personal, confidential, regulated, wallet, seed phrase, private-key, customer, or proprietary information. No account, cookie, storage, network request, wallet connection, token route, or transaction broadcast.

01

Mission manifest

No user data wanted. Use fictional or public-safe examples only.

02

Evidence-state controls

03

Routing decision

Awaiting router run.

Router family

Every router must emit the same contract.

Selected agents, role assignments, subtasks, access graph, tool permissions, budget, validators, stopping rule, escalation rule, proof requirements, rollback target, and public/private boundary.

Coordination law

Maximum effect is not more agents.

Maximum effect is verified useful work per unit of cost, latency, risk, and proof debt. The router is advisory until validators, evidence, risk, and human-review boundaries agree.

No validator, no stopping rule, no proof bundle — no route.
04

Chronicle trace
